verbatim: People in Sybaris, as it appears, have their invitations to women presented a year in advance so as to afford them plenty of time to provide themselves with clothes and jewellery to wear when they come to dinner
extracted_rules.reasoning: Thales describes a specific social convention in Sybaris where women are explicitly distinguished from men by requiring a one-year notice for dinner invitations to allow for aesthetic preparation.

verbatim: Yes, said Aesop, that is because you have not yet written a law that slaves shall not get drunk, which would be a similar law to fit this case, as at Athens you wrote a law that slaves shall not have any love-affair and shall not rub down like athletes.
extracted_rules.reasoning: Aesop refers to specific Athenian legal prohibitions targeting the group of slaves, specifically denying them the right to exercise like athletes or engage in recognized love affairs, while implying a normative prohibition on their drunkenness.

verbatim: as at Athens you wrote a law that slaves shall not have any love-affair and shall not rub down like athletes. A reason for the prohibition is given in Plutarch’s Life of Solon, chap. i. (p. 79 A).
extracted_rules.reasoning: The text explicitly mentions an Athenian law enacted by Solon that prevents slaves from performing the actions of athletes ('rub down'), which was a hallmark of free citizen education and status.

verbatim: Next Bias said, If he should be the very first to conform to his country’s laws.
extracted_rules.reasoning: In a discussion on the best form of government, Bias defines a rule of conduct for the group of rulers (kings/despots), asserting they must follow the laws of the state themselves rather than being above them.

verbatim: I think that a State succeeds best, and most effectively perpetuates democracy, in which persons uninjured by a crime, no less than the injured person, prosecute the criminal and get him punished.
extracted_rules.reasoning: Solon describes the fundamental legal principle of his Athenian democracy where the right to bring a legal action is granted to any citizen, not just the direct victim of a crime, to ensure communal protection.

_perseus_title: The Dinner of the Seven Wise Men
